hero.headline: DBX Solutions democratizes advanced technology for small and mid-sized businesses.
hero.body: We design, build, and run AI-powered customer experience for you—so you can keep up with how business moves today, without building a pricey in-house AI bench. We’ve spent 20+ years in CX and integrations; we plug automation into the helpdesk, CRM, and channels you already use, from WhatsApp and SMS to voice and web chat.
points: Your team spends less time on repeat questions. Automation handles the routine stuff; humans step in when it’s the right call.
Customers get answers faster on WhatsApp, SMS, voice, and messaging. We work with your stack—we’re not here to rip it out.
You ship something real in weeks, see if it’s working, then grow from there. No endless pilot theater.

value.equation: Speed × Clarity × Results you can see
value.noteText: The first workflow customers actually touch—running in your environment, tied to real tickets and data.

media.eyebrow: FROM FIRST WORKFLOW TO SOMETHING PEOPLE KEEP USING
media.title: A path your team can run without a manual the size of a phone book.
media.body: Pick one workflow that actually matters. Wire it to the tools you already have—WhatsApp, SMS, whatever your customers use. Get your front line comfortable. Then tighten it based on real conversations, not slide decks.
steps: Agree what “better” means and which systems we’re touching
Choose the first automation (small enough to ship)
Put it in production—not a demo sandbox
Train the people who talk to customers
Check the numbers monthly and adjust
media.caption: Small phases, real launches: ship something, learn, repeat.

trust.label: A few places we’ve helped teams like yours get unstuck:
industries: E-commerce brand juggling service and messaging
Regional clinics handling scheduling and reminders
B2B distributor with orders and logistics questions
Growing SaaS company sorting self-service and billing FAQs

services.title: Three ways we usually start
services.description: Nothing vague here—these are concrete offers. Each one ties into WhatsApp, SMS, voice, and messaging where you need them, and hooks into the CRM and helpdesk you’re already paying for.

cards.title: Customer Support Automation
cards.description: When the same questions eat your week, this is the lever. We route conversations across WhatsApp, SMS, web chat, and voice so people get answers fast, and your team only handles what actually needs a human.
cards.outputs: Flows ready for production, with escalation that makes sense
cards.artifacts: Hooked to helpdesk / CRM, FAQs, and tickets
cards.kpi: Hours back to the team every week
items: WhatsApp, SMS, messaging, and voice—use what your customers actually pick up
Answers pulled from your policies and knowledge base, not generic fluff
Rules your agents recognize so handoffs don’t feel like starting over

cards.title: AI Receptionist & Appointment Handling
cards.description: Missed calls and slow callbacks cost money. We set up flows that answer inbound traffic, figure out what someone needs, and book or move appointments on WhatsApp, SMS, and voice—so fewer leads quietly disappear.
cards.artifacts: Works with calendars and CRM fields you already rely on
cards.kpi: Shorter path from “interested” to “on the calendar”
items: After-hours coverage for the predictable stuff—you set the guardrails
SMS or WhatsApp nudges that match how your team actually works

cards.title: Lead Qualification & Conversion
cards.description: Sales shouldn’t live in triage. We help you grab leads on WhatsApp and messaging, sort who’s worth a call, and push clean data into the CRM so reps spend time on deals, not spreadsheet cleanup.
cards.artifacts: Plain definitions: what counts as qualified vs. nurture
cards.kpi: More inquiries turning into a real next step
items: Quick replies where buyers already are—WhatsApp and SMS included
Structured fields so follow-up isn’t a guessing game
Routing that matches how your territories or pods actually split work

growth.title: We stick around while your AI systems earn their keep
growth.intro: Going live is day one. Models drift, customer language shifts, and new ideas pop up. That’s normal.
growth.body1: We handle the maintenance and tuning so things don’t slowly rot. When the numbers look good, we help you stretch into new use cases and channels—the same way we’d build the first one: careful, tested, boring in the right way.
growth.body2: We care what happens three months in, not just launch week.
growth.body3: That’s how smaller teams keep up without hiring a whole internal AI org.

model.title: Start small. Add scope when it’s justified.
model.intro: We’ll agree on a first slice you can measure, with a monthly number you can plan around—no “we’ll figure it out later” meter running forever.
model.body1: Either we run it for you, or we train your people to run it. Your call.
model.body2: You know what you’re buying each phase and what finished looks like before we write the first line of config.
model.privacy: Don’t want to own day-to-day upkeep? We can run it under standard industry practice, and you’re not chained to us—switch vendors whenever you need to.

process.title: Three phases: talk, scope, ship—then keep it honest.
process.body: You get one person accountable end to end. We find where time leaks, pick an automation that pays for itself early, and wire it into your CRM, helpdesk, WhatsApp, SMS—whatever matters for your customers. Then we launch, train, and adjust from real tickets and messages.

steps.title: Discover & prioritize
steps.body: We talk to owners, leads, and the folks on the phone. We map the pain and pick one thing that should pay back fast—including which channels (WhatsApp, SMS, voice, messaging) deserve attention first.

steps.title: Design & scope
steps.body: Simple flows, a few success metrics, and a budget-sized scope. We fold in privacy and security expectations for your industry so there’s no surprise compliance lecture later.
steps.chip: Something you can sign off on

steps.title: Build, launch & coach
steps.body: We integrate, run a tight pilot, get your team comfortable, and tweak from real traffic—weekly at first, then on a rhythm that fits you.
steps.chip: Live in weeks, with backup when you need it

useCases.title: Scenarios we hear a lot
useCases.body: You don’t need a call center the size of a stadium to feel modern—you need the right automation in the right spots.
items: Handle support volume without adding headcount every quarter
Stop losing leads because nobody replied fast enough
Meet people on WhatsApp, SMS, voice, and messaging without juggling five tabs
Cover routine requests overnight without pretending you hired a night shift
Give straight answers when customers ask, so trust builds instead of eroding

proof.title: Numbers we track, plus people in their own words

Median time to first live workflow

testimonials.quote: We were drowning in repeat questions on email and chat. DBX connected our CRM and messaging in one flow—the team finally uses one playbook.
testimonials.author: Operations Manager

testimonials.quote: We shipped a working assistant in a few sprints and adjusted based on real tickets—not months of deck iterations.
testimonials.author: Founder

contact.title: Tell us what’s going on
contact.body: Who answers the phone today? Which channels matter—WhatsApp, SMS, something else? What tools are already in the mix? Drop that in the form. We’ll suggest a sensible first move, and we’ll get back to you within a business day.
contact.urgency: We only take a handful of deep-dive projects each quarter—ask us what the calendar looks like.
contact.bookingNote: Rather talk live? Grab a slot →
